label: Social Security Administration — Sex Designation
description: Update the sex designation on your Social Security records. This is tracked separately from the SSA name update because the two aspects have different legal statuses and requirements. The sex designation field on SSA records is currently not changeable under federal policy.
immutable_note: Federal executive orders issued in January 2025 directed the Social Security Administration to recognize only binary sex designations (M or F) and removed the X option that had been available since 2022. M/F changes now require documentation rather than self-certification. This policy is being challenged in court. This item will be updated as legal status changes.
immutable_compassion_note: This is a policy situation, not a permanent legal reality. Advocacy organizations including Lambda Legal and the ACLU are actively challenging these orders. If this matters to you, the advocacy links below are the best source of current status.

process.summary: When federal policy permits: sex designation changes at the SSA are made using the same Form SS-5 used for name changes. The current executive orders have suspended this for the X option and added documentation requirements for M/F changes.

steps.description: Monitor current policy through LGBTQ+ legal advocacy organizations. The legal landscape around this has changed rapidly and may change again.
steps.note: Lambda Legal, the ACLU, and the National Center for Transgender Equality publish updates on federal ID policy changes.

gender_marker_change.status_note: Federal executive orders issued in January 2025 directed the SSA to recognize only binary (M/F) sex designations. The X option is no longer available. M/F changes now require documentation — physician statement or court order — rather than the self-certification that had been policy since 2022. Active litigation may alter this. Do not attempt a sex designation change without first checking current guidance from an LGBTQ+ legal organization.

presence_level_content.some_guidance: This item is here so you know it exists and can track whether policy changes. You do not need to act on it now.
presence_level_content.walk_with_me: It is reasonable to feel frustrated that something that was possible is no longer available. Knowing where things stand — even when the answer is 'not right now' — is more useful than not tracking it.

sources.label: National Center for Transgender Equality: Federal ID Updates
